items.title: ephtracy voxel-model (+ stb)
items.what: observed ephtracy/voxel-model: reference .vox models + the MagicaVoxel .vox file-format documentation + .svo sparse-voxel-octree samples (ephtracy = author of MagicaVoxel, the free GPU voxel editor/renderer). "stb" refers to the single-head

items.title: github.com/Hmbown/CodeWhale
items.what: Real Rust coding-agent (38.7k stars, v0.8.62, 25+ providers) with a 4-layer nested constitution; law lives in the harness; runtime-validated .codewhale/constitution.json.

items.title: JamieMagee/wayback (GitHub Action)
items.what: GitHub Action (v2.2.0, rel 2026-04-16) that snapshots pages into the Internet Archive as part of CI/CD.

items.title: waybackurls + waybackpack
items.what: waybackurls audits archived URL coverage; waybackpack pulls local frozen copies of URLs.

items.title: gmcli (Minimal Gmail CLI)
items.what: observed badlogic/gmcli (Mario Zechner). Minimal Gmail CLI: search threads via Gmail query syntax, read threads (Message-ID, headers, body, attachments), manage labels/drafts/accounts via OAuth. NOTE (SR23 correction): the prior FINDING gro

items.title: The Subprime AI Crisis Is Here (Ed Zitron)
items.what: Long-form analysis arguing the AI industry is structurally subprime: labs burn far more compute than revenue (Anthropic ~5B rev vs ~10B compute), circular financing, rugpull began June 2025 priority-tiers and hit subscribers March 2026.

items.title: Old Software Was Fast + Computer latency 1977-2017
items.what: The lean-engineering pair (Aytas + Dan Luu): software was fast when it had no choice; latency has regressed despite faster hardware.

items.title: "Old Software Was Fast Because It Had No Choice"
items.what: observed Essay by Yusuf Aytas. Thesis: old software was fast not from virtue but because the machine said no; scarce RAM/CPU FORCED engineers to understand their service. Modern infra is forgiving, so waste survives long enough to look norm

items.title: Winhance
items.what: observed memstechtips/Winhance (PolyForm Shield license). C# Windows 10/11 debloat + optimize + customize panel (remove Xbox/Teams/OneDrive/telemetry, export revertible tweak scripts, build custom Win11 ISOs). NOTE (SR23): also prior-FINDIN

items.title: Best-README-Template
items.what: observed othneildrew/Best-README-Template. A widely-used README scaffold. NOTE (SR23): author is othneildrew, not asgeir.

items.title: GLM-5.2 (Built for Long-Horizon Tasks)
items.what: MIT-licensed 1M-context open-weights frontier model, ranks #2 behind Opus 4.8 per the loaded material.

items.title: CCEconReport Appendix G + H
items.what: inferred Anthropic Economic Index report material (the March-2026 "Learning curves" report is the live one). Public report confirms Claude Code run-rate revenue >$2.5B Feb 2026, coding migrating from claude.ai to first-party API. The specif

items.title: arxiv 2601.03267v2 = OpenAI GPT-5 System Card
items.what: GPT-5 system card: a fast model + a deeper reasoning model behind a real-time router trained on user-switch/preference/correctness signals.

items.title: arxiv 2508.09224v1 = From Hard Refusals to Safe-Completions (Vallone et al)
items.what: OpenAI safety-training paper centered on output safety rather than binary intent-classification.

items.title: arxiv 2502.18600v2 = Chain of Draft: Thinking Faster by Writing Less
items.what: Minimal intermediate reasoning; CoT-comparable accuracy at ~92% fewer tokens.

items.title: arxiv 2505.08775v1 = HealthBench (OpenAI)
items.what: 5000 physician-graded multi-turn health conversations as an LLM health eval.

items.title: wiki.pokeclicker.com
items.what: Pokemon idle-game wiki Dan praised for simple layout + clear interlinked world-building (hash-routed SPA; structure sourced from static render + DeepWiki mirror).

items.title: OrcaRouter-Lite
items.what: Self-hosted OpenAI-compatible LLM router, BYOK, single-workspace, streaming, managed safety net.
